name: Lunar Prospector
description: First NASA Discovery-class lunar mission, 1998-1999. Direct neutron-spectrometer detection of hydrogen at both lunar poles — strong evidence for water ice in permanently-shadowed crater floors. Deliberately impacted into Shoemaker crater at end of mission, hoping the impact ejecta would confirm water; Earth-based observers detected nothing definitive.
scale_fact: Polar orbit at 100 km altitude. Confirmed Clementine's polar-ice hypothesis; established neutron spectroscopy as the primary tool for lunar volatiles.
